title: Vendor Playbook Catalog
description: Per-vendor playbook entries built up as connectors land in Phase B. Each entry captures the patterns, sources, and constraints a future re-extraction of the same (or similar) vendor can leverage for ~60-80% cost reduction. Per architecture §4.16.

ResponseEnvelope.description: Top-level data key under which list responses are wrapped (e.g., 'results', 'data', 'records', 'items', or '' for raw-array responses).

DocsScrapingStrategy.description: Which strategy library entry was used (e.g., 'openapi-3.x-repo', 'jsforce-typedef-parser', 'stoplight-scrape'). Empty when the vendor uses a unique one-off parser.

TemplateVarPattern.description: How URL template variables relate to IO PKs (e.g., '/parents/{ParentID}/children → children.ParentID FK'). Captured by IOIOFExtractor.

RateLimitNotes.description: Free-form rate-limit summary (window, scope, special quotas).

PrimaryKeyConvention.description: Vendor's PK naming convention (from MJ: Integrations.PrimaryKeyFieldName + Confidence).

VerifiedPatterns.description: Names of entries in .verified-patterns/ that this vendor exercises. Empty until phase C.

LastVerifiedAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful build of this vendor's connector.

LiveTestedAgainst.description: Tier identifiers that passed against live API (e.g., ['T0','T1','T2','T3','T4','T9','T10']). Empty when no live test infrastructure available.

KnownConstraints.description: Constraints captured during build (from additionalSchemaInfo.json) that future re-builds should be aware of.

FailurePatternsResolvedHere.description: Names of .failure-patterns/ entries this connector demonstrates resolution of (e.g., 'FP-001' for OAuth refresh-token rotation handled via OAuth2TokenManager).

Quirks.description: Vendor-specific peculiarities surfaced during extraction (per directive §29).
